entries.title: DLMS UA and OpenADR Alliance sign global liaison to bridge smart-meter data and grid-edge flexibility signalling
entries.summary: The two standards bodies announced a liaison agreement to map COSEM (smart-meter data exchange) to OpenADR (DR/DER signalling), coordinate certifications, and explore DLMS/COSEM transport over OpenADR. The practical outcome for vendors: one less bespoke integration layer between meters, HEMS, and flexibility services.
keywords: DLMS UA and OpenADR Alliance sign global liaison to bridge smart-meter data and grid-edge flexibility signalling

The two standards bodies announced a liaison agreement to map COSEM (smart-meter data exchange) to OpenADR (DR/DER signalling), coordinate certifications, and explore DLMS/COSEM transport over OpenADR. The practical outcome for vendors: one less bespoke integration layer between meters, HEMS, and flexibility services.
Direct fit for both brands — Flux's HEMS/VPP architecture sits exactly on this seam; a published mapping reduces integration cost for GrooveGrid sub-metering and HarmonyGrid DR participation
industrialnews.co.uk
both
Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0 GA — Semantic Kernel + AutoGen converge under MIT license with native MCP and A2A interop

Microsoft shipped Agent Framework 1.0 on April 3 for both .NET and Python (package `Microsoft.Agents.AI` / `microsoft-agents-ai`), unifying 75K+ GitHub stars of prior work. Stable APIs, long-term support commitment, first-class MCP tool discovery, and A2A 1.0 for cross-framework agent collaboration. DevUI debugger visualizes agent execution and tool calls in real time.

Pilot pattern: replace ad-hoc LangChain/CrewAI glue with a 1.0-stable SDK for enterprise-grade multi-agent orchestration. MIT license and dual-runtime support make it a safe default for Microsoft-centric stacks through 2027
devblogs.microsoft.com
enterprise
VPPs must scale in 2026 or lose ground to traditional generation — Wood Mackenzie flags broadening-without-deepening gap

North American VPP capacity reached 37.5 GW (Wood Mackenzie), but year-over-year growth was only 13.7% while program counts grew over 33%. The market is broadening faster than it's deepening. PJM raised ELCC for demand-side resources from 69% to 92% in its 2027-28 auction, and FERC continues reviewing Order 2222 compliance. Emerald AI is adding distribution-system DER to its platform in 2026.

HarmonyGrid's multi-unit landlord SaaS pitch benefits when wholesale compensation improves — ELCC bump and Order 2222 compliance reduce the 'will I get paid for this?' objection. Property managers who enroll early capture higher capacity payments
utilitydive.com
harmony
2026 AI property management buyer's guide shows 20–35% operating cost reduction — predictive maintenance + energy management lead ROI

The AI Consulting Network's 2026 guide finds best-in-class AI PM platforms cut operating costs 20–35% through automation. Predictive maintenance alone cuts emergency repairs 40–60% and extends equipment life 15–25%. AI energy management (weather, occupancy, rate structure, equipment analysis) is now expected for ESG reporting, not a nice-to-have. Smaller operators (50–5,000 units) can access enterprise-grade capabilities via cloud platforms.

GrooveGrid's core positioning — sub-metered small/mid multi-tenant buildings — is the exact segment now accessing AI PM features. Lead with predictive HVAC + energy reporting as the double-barrel ROI pitch
theaiconsultingnetwork.com
groove
For Researchers: Open-weights wave in April — Qwen 3, Gemma 4, Mistral Medium 3, Llama 4 Scout/Maverick, DeepSeek R2, GLM-5.1

April 2026 shipped the largest open-weights batch in months. Qwen 3 (Apr 8, 0.6B–72B, dual thinking/fast modes, Apache 2.0) is the new default for agent loops. GLM-5.1 (Apr 7, 744B MoE, MIT) reportedly beats Claude Opus 4.6 and GPT-5.4 on SWE-Bench Pro. DeepSeek R2 hits AIME 92.7% at ~70% lower inference cost than frontier cloud. All runnable in Ollama/LM Studio today.
whatllm.org
harmony
Agent governance: Microsoft open-sources toolkit that hooks into LangChain, CrewAI, Google ADK, and its own framework

Microsoft shipped an Agent Governance Toolkit as a monorepo of seven independently installable packages. Sits on existing extension points (LangChain callbacks, CrewAI task decorators, ADK plugin system, Agent Framework middleware) so teams don't need to rewrite agents to add policy, audit, and control.
helpnetsecurity.com
enterprise
Self-Hosted: Sovereign local stack recipe (Ollama 5.x + Open WebUI + pgvector on Ubuntu 24.04) hits HN front page

End-to-end tested playbook for a fully local AI stack — zero cloud, zero API costs — with Ollama 5.x as inference, Open WebUI (45K+ stars) as the chat/RAG layer, and pgvector for embeddings. The stack matches GGUF models between Ollama, LM Studio, and Jan AI without re-downloading. [home-lab ready, SMB-friendly]
news.ycombinator.com
both
Free Tools: One-click local LLM installer comparison — Ollama vs LM Studio vs Jan AI vs GPT4All (April 2026)

All four install in under 5 minutes, all use llama.cpp under the hood, and all share GGUF format. Ollama (port 11434, MIT, OpenAI-compatible API) is the developer pick; Open WebUI via Docker turns it into a private ChatGPT for teams; Jan AI wins on privacy (no telemetry, fully offline). Free tier: all of them.
promptquorum.com
enterprise
Creator Tools: Faith-based music workflows with Suno, Udio, Soundverse — genre-aware worship song generation and multilingual output

Soundverse's AI Song Generator added Pop Worship, Gospel, Folk, and Orchestral Praise presets with multilingual output across 10+ languages. For worship leaders, the workflow is: lyrics in → style + tempo → male/female vocal → stems exportable for a live band to play over. Pairs with lalal.ai for stem separation and CapCut for lyric reels.
soundverse.ai
bonus

entries.title: AI-Based HVAC Optimization Delivers 20–30% Savings with Sub-2-Year Payback
entries.summary: New commercial real estate case studies confirm AI-driven HVAC control cuts energy use by 20–30% in mixed-use buildings, with payback periods under 24 months. Predictive scheduling and occupancy-sensing are the primary levers — a direct match for GrooveGrid's positioning in small multi-tenant properties.
keywords: AI-Based HVAC Optimization Delivers 20–30% Savings with Sub-2-Year Payback

New commercial real estate case studies confirm AI-driven HVAC control cuts energy use by 20–30% in mixed-use buildings, with payback periods under 24 months. Predictive scheduling and occupancy-sensing are the primary levers — a direct match for GrooveGrid's positioning in small multi-tenant properties.
Strong fit: GrooveGrid small-building HVAC module

Generative AI Customer Support Agents Now Deflect 40–60% of Tier-1 B2B Tickets

Enterprise SaaS vendors are deploying LLM-powered support copilots that resolve routine queries without human escalation, cutting support costs 30–45%. Internal knowledge-base grounding and structured escalation logic are emerging as the key differentiators.
General enterprise trend — AI operations automation
